00029 
00030 /*
00031  * VFS operations involving the current directory.
00032  */
00033 
00034 #include <types.h>
00035 #include <kern/errno.h>
00036 #include <stat.h>
00037 #include <lib.h>
00038 #include <uio.h>
00039 #include <thread.h>
00040 #include <current.h>
00041 #include <vfs.h>
00042 #include <fs.h>
00043 #include <vnode.h>
00044 
00045 /*
00046  * Get current directory as a vnode.
00047  * 
00048  * We do not synchronize curthread->t_cwd, because it belongs exclusively
00049  * to its own thread; no other threads should access it.
00050  */
00051 int
00052 vfs_getcurdir(struct vnode **ret)
00053 {
00054         int rv = 0;
00055 
00056         if (curthread->t_cwd!=NULL) {
00057                 VOP_INCREF(curthread->t_cwd);
00058                 *ret = curthread->t_cwd;
00059         }
00060         else {
00061                 rv = ENOENT;
00062         }
00063 
00064         return rv;
00065 }
00066 
00067 /*
00068  * Set current directory as a vnode.
00069  * The passed vnode must in fact be a directory.
00070  */
00071 int
00072 vfs_setcurdir(struct vnode *dir)
00073 {
00074         struct vnode *old;
00075         mode_t vtype;
00076         int result;
00077 
00078         result = VOP_GETTYPE(dir, &vtype);
00079         if (result) {
00080                 return result;
00081         }
00082         if (vtype != S_IFDIR) {
00083                 return ENOTDIR;
00084         }
00085 
00086         VOP_INCREF(dir);
00087 
00088         old = curthread->t_cwd;
00089         curthread->t_cwd = dir;
00090 
00091         if (old!=NULL) { 
00092                 VOP_DECREF(old);
00093         }
00094 
00095         return 0;
00096 }
00097 
00098 /*
00099  * Set current directory to "none".
00100  */
00101 int
00102 vfs_clearcurdir(void)
00103 {
00104         struct vnode *old;
00105 
00106         old = curthread->t_cwd;
00107         curthread->t_cwd = NULL;
00108 
00109         if (old!=NULL) { 
00110                 VOP_DECREF(old);
00111         }
00112 
00113         return 0;
00114 }
00115 
00116 /*
00117  * Set current directory, as a pathname. Use vfs_lookup to translate
00118  * it to a vnode.
00119  */
00120 int
00121 vfs_chdir(char *path)
00122 {
00123         struct vnode *vn;
00124         int result;
00125 
00126         result = vfs_lookup(path, &vn);
00127         if (result) {
00128                 return result;
00129         }
00130         result = vfs_setcurdir(vn);
00131         VOP_DECREF(vn);
00132         return result;
00133 }
00134 
00135 /*
00136  * Get current directory, as a pathname.
00137  * Use VOP_NAMEFILE to get the pathname and FSOP_GETVOLNAME to get the
00138  * volume name.
00139  */
00140 int
00141 vfs_getcwd(struct uio *uio)
00142 {
00143         struct vnode *cwd;
00144         int result;
00145         const char *name;
00146         char colon=':';
00147 
00148         KASSERT(uio->uio_rw==UIO_READ);
00149 
00150         result = vfs_getcurdir(&cwd);
00151         if (result) {
00152                 return result;
00153         }
00154 
00155         /* The current dir must be a directory, and thus it is not a device. */
00156         KASSERT(cwd->vn_fs != NULL);
00157 
00158         name = FSOP_GETVOLNAME(cwd->vn_fs);
00159         if (name==NULL) {
00160                 vfs_biglock_acquire();
00161                 name = vfs_getdevname(cwd->vn_fs);
00162                 vfs_biglock_release();
00163         }
00164         KASSERT(name != NULL);
00165 
00166         result = uiomove((char *)name, strlen(name), uio);
00167         if (result) {
00168                 goto out;
00169         }
00170         result = uiomove(&colon, 1, uio);
00171         if (result) {
00172                 goto out;
00173         }
00174 
00175         result = VOP_NAMEFILE(cwd, uio);
00176 
00177  out:
00178 
00179         VOP_DECREF(cwd);
00180         return result;
00181 }
